title: Launch Academy profile ties together Ph.D., Beckman, and Canada rationale
excerpt: The 2018 Launch Academy / Maple profile is one of the most useful public documents because it links Sean Xiang's CUHK doctorate, Beckman Laser Institute role, and the strategic case for expanding into Canada.
caption: This functions less like decorative press coverage and more like a connective archival document between the academic chapter and later geographic expansion.

title: OpenStack community release is the earliest strong public company anchor
excerpt: The February 2013 OpenStack announcement is more than an ecosystem badge. It publicly names Sean Xiang as co-founder and CEO while positioning Bloombase around enterprise cloud privacy, compliance, and data-at-rest protection.
caption: Instead of pretending to have exact incorporation paperwork, the chronicle can use this dated release as the first hard public proof that the founder chapter was already legible in market-facing language.
visualText: 2013-02-18 · Sean Xiang is quoted as co-founder and CEO while the product is framed as the missing security layer for OpenStack storage.
quoteText: One of the key challenges with enterprise core business applications on cloud adoption is the need to achieve data privacy and regulatory compliance.

title: The VMware alliance shows Bloombase entering enterprise infrastructure fast
excerpt: The August 2013 VMware Technology Alliance announcement tightens the founding chapter because it shows Bloombase speaking directly to virtualization and cloud buyers within months of the earliest visible 2013 public trail.
caption: Editorially, this is the right kind of founding-era artifact: concrete, dated, and structurally aligned with the long-term thesis that Bloombase was built inside trust-heavy infrastructure markets from the beginning.
visualText: 2013-08-30 · A direct company URL ties Bloombase to VMware's alliance program and to virtualization-era data privacy concerns.
quoteText: Data privacy tops one of CIOs' concerns over virtualization and cloud.

title: VMware alliance marks the start of a visible enterprise-validation run
excerpt: The January 2014 Bloombase announcement about joining VMware's Technology Alliance Partner Program gives the middle chapter a concrete starting marker in platform interoperability and ecosystem trust.
caption: One announcement is not the whole story, but it helps establish the cadence of repeated validation signals that follow.

title: VMware Cloud on AWS shows the company translating into the cloud stack
excerpt: The 2018 StoreSafe for VMware Cloud on AWS announcement matters because it shows the validation story evolving beyond on-prem credibility into a cloud-era deployment surface.
caption: This is the kind of artifact that keeps the middle chapter from reading like a static partner list: the platform context keeps changing, and Bloombase keeps trying to stay legible inside it.

title: The NVIDIA DPU / GPU post reframes Bloombase for accelerated computing
excerpt: Bloombase's 2023 post on rebuilding data-at-rest security with NVIDIA DPUs and GPUs is the clearest public signal that the company is trying to translate long-built security primitives into an AI-era stack.
caption: Editorially, this is the bridge between legacy enterprise credibility and present-tense AI relevance.

title: The Utimaco alliance gives the AI-era chapter a direct primary source
excerpt: The April 2025 Bloombase announcement explicitly frames the Utimaco relationship around data security solutions for the AI era, making it a stronger anchor than generic conference visibility alone.
caption: This is the kind of source that can justify richer future media treatment because it is direct, dated, and specific.

title: Dell certification turns interoperability into dated proof, not generic enterprise name-dropping
excerpt: The January 2014 Dell certification release is a useful editorial anchor because it shows Bloombase presenting compatibility and certification in specific enterprise infrastructure terms at the start of the long validation run.
caption: This is what keeps the middle chapter archival instead of repetitive: the story is told through concrete documents that mark how trust was earned, one platform relationship at a time.
visualText: 2014-01-22 - A dated Bloombase release frames enterprise compatibility as a concrete certification milestone rather than a vague customer-proof claim.

title: The Hitachi alliance shows the validation story widening across storage infrastructure
excerpt: By late 2014, the source trail is not just repeating one partner category. The Hitachi Data Systems alliance expands the public record into another major enterprise-storage environment, reinforcing the pattern of infrastructure-grade interoperability.
caption: Editorially, this matters because the middle chapter becomes a sequence of distinct trust surfaces: certification, alliance, HSM integration, marketplace distribution, and security validation.
visualText: 2014-11-18 - Bloombase positions itself inside another enterprise storage stack, adding breadth to the validation trail.

title: IBM security validation gives the chapter a stronger mid-period maturity marker
excerpt: The IBM Ready for Security Intelligence milestone is valuable because it reads like a later-stage trust signal rather than an early alliance experiment. It helps the chapter show progression from initial ecosystem entry to sustained enterprise validation.
caption: Placed midstream, this card changes the rhythm of the chapter: the evidence is not all clustered in the founding years, and the company remains legible as infrastructure markets evolve.
visualText: 2017-03-20 - A direct Bloombase release marks a more mature stage of the company's enterprise-security validation arc.

title: Azure Marketplace proves the validation story reaches public-cloud distribution, not only private infrastructure
excerpt: The 2019 Azure Marketplace listing is one of the most important middle-chapter documents because it shows the company translating its security posture into a public-cloud buying and deployment surface.
caption: This card helps the chronology feel less like a frozen on-prem era. The validation chapter becomes a moving sequence from storage and alliance proof into cloud-era distribution.
visualText: 2019-01-22 - Bloombase appears in a public-cloud marketplace, extending the validation arc beyond classic enterprise data-center partnerships.

title: Canada expansion lands inside a real multi-office footprint, not a symbolic map pin
excerpt: The strongest version of the geography chapter is not that Bloombase "went global," but that Vancouver becomes legible alongside Redwood City, Hong Kong, and Frankfurt on a direct company offices page while the Launch Academy profile explains why Canada mattered.
caption: This is the right editorial treatment for the map chapter: one source explains the strategic why, another proves the operating footprint. Together they let the geography module read like evidence instead of atmosphere.
visualText: Redwood City remains the company spine while Vancouver becomes a source-backed expansion chapter connected to talent, cryptography, and ecosystem positioning.

footprintItems.detail: Science Park presence keeps the Asia-Pacific thread visible

footprintItems.label: Frankfurt

title: The recent chapter reads as a stacked signal set: accelerated computing, public visibility, then alliance execution
excerpt: Instead of treating the AI era like one more press-release card, the chronicle can show the sequence: a 2023 NVIDIA reframing, a still-active public news surface, and a 2025 Utimaco alliance that explicitly names the AI era.
caption: That pattern matters because it keeps the latest section from feeling like nostalgia dressed up as AI. The chapter has a real through-line from technical argument to ecosystem presence to named alliance.

signalItems.title: NVIDIA DPU / GPU reframing
signalItems.text: Bloombase explicitly translates data-at-rest security into accelerated-computing language.

signalItems.title: Public news surface still active
signalItems.text: The Bloombase news index keeps the release trail visible instead of forcing the page to rely on vague "recently active" claims.

signalItems.title: Utimaco alliance for the AI era
signalItems.text: A direct alliance announcement names AI-era data security as the current market frame.
